questions.text: The SI unit of electric current is:
questions.explanation: Ampere (A) is the SI unit of electric current. 1 Ampere = 1 Coulomb per second.
Ohm's law states that the potential difference (V) across a conductor is directly proportional to the current (I) flowing through it: V = IR.

questions.text: The resistance of a conductor is directly proportional to its ________.
questions.explanation: Resistance R = ρL/A, where ρ is resistivity, L is length, A is cross-sectional area. So R ∝ L.

questions.correctAnswer: Drift velocity is the average velocity acquired by free electrons in a conductor under the influence of an electric field.
questions.explanation: Drift velocity (vd) is the slow average velocity of electrons in a conductor when an electric field is applied. It's typically of the order of mm/s.
